summary: The test suite is comprehensive and well-organized, covering all major requirements: INI parsing (sections, comments, multi-line, quoting, equals-in-values), type coercion (all boolean synonyms, int/long/float/string fallback, schema-directed), schema validation (required keys, type checking, multiple errors, global section), schema loading (type aliases), JSON output (type coercion, nested sections, schema influence), and YAML output (literal block scalars, special-char quoting, indentation). Parametrized Theory tests are used effectively for boolean/type coverage. Edge cases are solid: duplicate keys/sections, Windows line endings, empty input, inline comments, large integers, scientific notation, optional missing keys. Gaps that prevent a perfect rigor score: no negative/exception tests for malformed schema JSON; SchemaLoader.LoadFromFile is untested (only LoadFromJson); no Unicode or very long-value tests; YAML assertions are substring-matching which is moderately brittle for format changes; no tests exercising the top-level CLI entry point. The EdgeCaseTests file mixes concerns (parser + schema + converter + integration) but includes a valuable full-pipeline test. Overall this is a trustworthy suite that would catch the vast majority of regressions against the stated requirements.
